name: Chicken Madeira on Herbed Biscuits
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Chicken breast, skin removed and boneless
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the chicken into 1-inch cubes.
2. In a 12-inch skillet, cook the chicken in hot oil over medium-high heat for 4 to 5 minutes, or until no longer pink. Remove the chicken and set aside.
3. Add garlic, mushrooms, and onion to the skillet. Cook, uncovered, for 4 to 5 minutes, or until liquid evaporates.
4. In a bowl, stir together sour cream, flour, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 teaspoon pepper.
5. Add the sour cream mixture, milk, and broth to the skillet. Cook and stir until bubbly. Cook for an additional minute.
6. Add the chicken and Madeira or sherry; heat through.
7. Serve over Herbed Biscuits. Sprinkle with thinly sliced green onions if desired. Makes 6 servings.
HERBED BISCUITS:
1. In a mixing bowl, stir together 1 3/4 cups packaged biscuit mix, 2 tablespoons thinly sliced green onion, and 1/2 teaspoon crushed, dried oregano.
2. Add 1/2 cup skim milk; stir just until the dough clings together.
3. On a floured surface, knead the dough 10 to 12 strokes.
4. Roll the dough to a 1/2-inch thickness.
5. Using a 2-inch biscuit cutter, cut the dough into 12 biscuits.
6. Reroll any trimmings as necessary.
7. Bake the biscuits on an ungreased baking sheet in a 425-degree oven for about 12 minutes.
